skill.name: tool-interface-analysis
skill.description: Analyze how agent frameworks define, register, validate, and execute tools, including schema generation, built-in tool inventories, error feedback, and retry behavior.
skill.summary: Map and compare tool interfaces in AI agent frameworks.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium findings, but review found them in Markdown examples and analysis templates, not executable skill code. The skill is low risk because it asks the assistant to inspect repository files and save an analysis document, which is expected for its purpose.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Expected During Analysis
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to examine framework source files and save a Markdown report. This is legitimate for a code analysis skill, but users should run it only on repositories they are permitted to inspect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The document explicitly names an output path and file patterns to examine. The behavior is normal for this skill and does not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The many command execution and weak-crypto alerts occur in fenced Markdown examples and placeholder analysis templates. They describe tool-interface patterns rather than code that this skill runs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are inside Markdown code fences or tables that document framework patterns. No separate executable script, shell hook, or installation code is present.

low_findings.title: API Key Mentions Are Placeholder Documentation
low_findings.description: The env_access alerts reference placeholder api_key fields in toolkit examples. No environment variable read, network transmission, or credential collection instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines use ellipses and placeholder api_key strings in examples. They do not access process environment variables or request real secrets.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Finding Is an Illustrative Pattern
low_findings.description: The importlib example explains discovery-based tool registration. It is not invoked by the skill and does not load modules in the marketplace package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is fenced Markdown under a section that compares registration approaches. There is no callable script or runtime entry point that executes the import.

content.value_statement: Agent frameworks expose tools in different ways, which makes comparison slow and inconsistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured audit of tool models, schemas, registration, execution, and feedback.
actual_capabilities: Identifies how a framework represents tools through classes, protocols, decorators, or models.
Compares schema generation methods such as introspection, Pydantic, decorators, and manual definitions.
Maps tool registration, discovery, factory, and toolkit patterns in source code.
Documents tool execution, validation, error feedback, retry, and self-correction behavior.
Produces a structured Markdown report for architecture comparison work.
limitations: It does not run benchmark tests or verify runtime behavior by itself.
It depends on readable source code and clear framework organization.
It may need another skill to map large codebases before deep analysis.
It does not replace a full security review of tool execution sandboxes.

use_cases.title: Compare Agent Frameworks
use_cases.description: Create consistent tool-interface notes across several frameworks before choosing an architecture.

use_cases.title: Review Tool Calling Design
use_cases.description: Audit how an internal framework validates tool inputs and returns errors to the model.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate a structured report that explains tool abstractions, schemas, registration, and execution flow.

prompt_templates.title: Map One Framework
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis of a single repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the tool interface for this framework. Focus on tool models, schema generation, registration, execution, and error feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Schema Methods
prompt_templates.prompt: Review how this framework turns tool definitions into JSON Schema. Compare introspection, Pydantic, decorator, and manual patterns if present.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Error Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of validation and retry behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the full path from invalid tool arguments to the message returned to the LLM. Include retry and self-correction behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build Comparison Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this framework for a comparative matrix. Include evidence-backed notes on tool abstraction, schema generation, registration, execution, parallelism, and design trade-offs.

output_examples.input: Analyze the tool interface in an agent framework repository.
output_examples.output: A Markdown report with summary fields, core abstraction notes, schema generation method, registration flow, execution behavior, and code references.

output_examples.input: Compare how two frameworks generate tool schemas.
output_examples.output: A concise comparison of automatic and manual schema strategies, including maintenance risks, validation quality, and implementation evidence.

output_examples.input: Investigate tool error handling and retry logic.
output_examples.output: A trace of validation failures, execution errors, feedback returned to the model, and retry or fallback mechanisms.

best_practices: Run a codebase mapping pass first when the repository is large or unfamiliar.
Capture file and line evidence for each architectural claim.
Separate documented capability from verified implementation behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not infer runtime behavior from README text alone.
Do not mix provider wire-format analysis with framework tool-interface analysis.
Do not ignore validation and error feedback when evaluating tool quality.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes how agent frameworks define, register, validate, execute, and report errors for tools.

faq.question: Does it execute framework tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill guides source analysis and report writing. It does not provide an executable runner.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect a structured Markdown report with summaries, evidence, tables, and architecture implications.

faq.question: Is this a security testing skill?
faq.answer: No. It can document sandbox and validation behavior, but it is not a full security assessment.

faq.question: When should I use another skill first?
faq.answer: Use codebase mapping first when you need to locate tool-related files in a large repository.
